summary: Shapiro continues his review of eCabinet Systems software, documenting his experience working through its extensive 350+ page tutorial. While impressed by the system's flexibility for designing cabinets with sheet goods, he identifies a practical gap: the software doesn't adequately address how to finish exposed plywood edges. He notes ongoing crash issues but remains cautiously optimistic, withholding final judgment until he produces an actual cabinet with the tool.
key_points: eCabinet Systems offers impressive flexibility despite stability issues and a steep learning curve
The software has a blind spot around finishing details like exposed plywood edges — edgebanding, veneer, or solid wood edging
Real validation of design software comes from producing a physical result, not just mastering the interface
money_quotes: It does still have bad problems with crash-prone-ness, but saving frequently solves most of that.
What the heck do you do with all the exposed plywood edges?
The real proof is when I produce my first cabinet with it.
key_insight: Design software is only as good as its ability to address real-world fabrication details — the gap between a nice digital model and a finished physical object is where tools prove their worth.
